content.description:
Multi-device sync and multi-user collaboration.

## Offline/Online Architecture

The system is fully offline today. Online capabilities could add:

- **Optional online enhancements**: Fetch web pages, import from APIs, check for updates
- **Cached web resources**: Fetch and cache URLs locally for offline access
- **Device sync**: Sync between devices (laptop, phone)
- **Collaborative editing**: Multiple users sharing items (much later)

## Sync Design Questions

| Question | Options |
|----------|---------|
| Conflict resolution | Last-write-wins, manual merge, CRDT |
| What syncs | All items, specific containers, user-selected |
| When to sync | Continuous, periodic, manual |
| Mobile subset | Full mirror or filtered subset |

## Collaborative Editing

Far-future consideration:
- Operational transforms or CRDT?
- Permissions model (per-item? per-container?)
- Real-time vs async collaboration

## Priority

Low — the offline-first architecture is a deliberate design choice. Sync adds significant complexity (conflict resolution, networking, auth). Consider only when multi-device use becomes a pain point.
